title: 1.1: Shows the observed step-by-step trace of what happens from raw bytes entering DNS layer to fully resolved name emerging (e.g., offset-by-offset decompression showing each label extraction and pointer following)
1.2: Identifies where decompression begins in the parsing process (e.g., DNSStrField.getfield calling dns_get_str).
1.3: Explains how domain names are encoded into length-prefixed label sequences (e.g., length byte followed by ASCII characters, zero byte terminator).
1.4: Shows how the 0xc0 marker distinguishes a compression pointer from a normal label length (e.g., high two bits set to 11, values 0x00-0x3f for labels, 0xc0+ for pointers).
1.5: Reports what happens when loop detection triggers (e.g., warning emitted, parsing loop breaks, partial results returned)

1.6: Explains that abs() in the bounds check catches negative pointer values resulting from small offsets after header subtraction (e.g., abs(pointer) >= max_length check, log_runtime.info for premature end)
1.7: Reports how truncated data is handled (e.g., incomplete pointer check, graceful degradation, returning partial results)
1.8: Identifies the InheritOriginDNSStrPacket class for maintaining access to full packet bytes (e.g., _orig_s attribute storing original packet, switching to s_full when needed)
1.9: Explains how the possible_shortens function generates compression candidates (e.g., iterating through all suffix parts of a domain name)
1.10: Explains how the compression algorithm performs pointer substitution after finding duplicates (e.g., building uncompressed first, scanning for duplicates, replacing with pointers)
1.11: Explains how identical domains with different compression strategies decompress to identical strings (e.g., test showing compressed and uncompressed versions produce same final values)
